title: Provenance + citation-coverage + staleness fail-safe enforcement
context: Financial figures, rates, thresholds, and statistics decay over time. A common failure mode is a figure silently going stale — still served as current while the underlying data has changed. The project's staleness fail-safe makes this a visible, gated event: each source's lastVerified/validUntil fields drive an automated check that flags or withholds any claim past its validUntil date until re-verified and re-signed-off. Citation coverage enforcement ensures no claim ships without an attached source. Together these form the runtime provenance guardrails.
objective: Implement and wire into CI: (1) a citation-coverage check that fails if any claim lacks an attached source, and (2) a staleness fail-safe that auto-flags or withholds any time-sensitive figure served past its validUntil date until re-verified and re-signed-off.
acceptanceCriteria: Citation-coverage check: no lesson or glossary entry can ship (CI passes) with an uncited factual claim; coverage check runs as part of `pnpm test` or `pnpm validate`
Staleness check: for every source with a validUntil date, CI flags (or withholds from the build output) any claim where today's date exceeds validUntil — not silently served as current
The staleness flag triggers a re-verification and re-sign-off requirement before the claim can be re-published; the process is documented
Sign-off is version-scoped: edits to a lesson invalidate its current sign-off and require re-review before re-publishing
Staleness test suite asserts that a claim with a past validUntil is correctly flagged and not served
Check is runnable locally and produces actionable output identifying which claim/source is stale or uncited
output: A merged PR adding the citation-coverage check and staleness fail-safe scripts (scripts/check-citations.ts and scripts/check-staleness.ts) wired into CI, with a test suite and documentation.
